evals.name: standard-weekly-audit
evals.prompt: Audit the three external-facing Phoenix skills (phoenix-tracing, phoenix-cli, phoenix-evals) against the last 7 days of changes on origin/main and patch anything stale or missing. Apply the edits in place — don't just produce a report.
evals.expected_output: Edits applied only inside .agents/skills/phoenix-tracing/, phoenix-cli/, or phoenix-evals/. A run summary covering edits, skipped commits, and out-of-scope findings. No invented APIs.

evals.name: casual-up-to-date-check
evals.prompt: Are the phoenix skills (tracing, cli, evals) up to date with what we shipped recently? If not, fix them.
evals.expected_output: Same as above — agent should produce a working audit even from a casual phrasing. Should not require the user to spell out every step.

evals.name: targeted-cli-audit
evals.prompt: I want to make sure phoenix-cli reflects any new commands or flags we shipped recently. Audit the last 7 days and patch the skill.
evals.expected_output: Agent focuses on phoenix-cli but still scans relevant CLI/client/API changes. Doesn't waste time editing phoenix-tracing or phoenix-evals unless there's overlap. Notes overlap if any.
